extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won from a 40-rider breakaway after teammate TRENTIN bridged solo on Orquiet d'Ason, then Yates used smaller gear to kick from technical final corner.
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott planted two riders in 40-man break; TRENTIN bridged solo on Orquiet d'Ason creating numbers advantage for finale.
YATES deliberately used smaller gear than rivals on technical finish allowing superior acceleration from final corner at lower cadence.
SAGAN attacked twice for intermediate sprint points despite break being 6:30 ahead, then immediately abandoned chase revealing green jersey calculation.
Peloton conceded 9:07 to break day before time trial, signaling GC teams prioritizing next day over policing stage.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Former track champion converted technical positioning skills into road stage win using gear selection tactics
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Solo bridged to leaders on Orquiet d'Ason showing all-rounder ability beyond expected sprint-only profile
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Attacked for intermediate points then quit break immediately, purely mathematical green jersey play with no stage ambition
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Defended yellow by staying front of peloton on climbs despite tomorrow's time trial, showing confidence not conservation
